images.title: France-001928 - View from the Rock of the Virgin (15741057912).jpg
images.description: Looking back to the shore from the Rock of the Virgin

images.title: France-001971 - Last View of the Beach (15120344743).jpg
images.description: Plage Miramar Beach, was a great walk and beach.

images.title: France-001967 - Saint-Martin Lighthouse (15119764604).jpg
images.description:
I just liked this view because of the large rock in front....

The Saint-Martin lighthouse was built in 1834 and is located at the northernmost point of Biarritz. The 73 meter (240ft) high lighthouse is open to the public, however, to get to the top you will have to climb all 248 steps.

This is one of the lighthouses designed by Augustin Fresnel himself; it commands a magnificent view of the Atlantic on one side and the Pyrénées mountains on the other. The keeper's house is staffed by a resident keeper. Located on the north side of the resort city of Biarritz, at the northern end of the popular Plage Miramar beach.

The light is still active; two white flashes every 10 sec. 47m (154ft) round masonry tower with lantern and gallery, rising from the center of a 2-story octagonal keeper's house; clamshell Fresnel lens (1904). Tower and keeper's house painted white, lantern and gallery black.

images.title: France-001964 - Plage Miramar Beach (15739688095).jpg
images.description:
Grande Plage de Biarritz.
Plage Miramar Beach on the way back to try and find the bus.

images.title: France-001966 - Shower Time (15554891310).jpg
images.description: I like the showers that are all along the beach so people can wash off the salt water after a swim. She is nice to have on the beach also.

images.title: France-001913 - Beach near Rock of the Virgin (15120044493).jpg
images.description:
The Rock of the Virgin is connected to the mainland via a bridge that was commissioned by Napoleon III. The bridge crosses over the water and partly through the rock that was carved out. 
The wooden bridge that was originally here did not withstand the test of time and Gustav Eiffel replaced it in 1887 with the iron bridge that is still in place.

images.title: France-001934 (15119597214).jpg
images.description: The Fishermen Port ("Port des Pêcheurs" in French) was built in 1870 for inshore fishermen. Today, most of the fishermen are gone and what is left is a cute little port mainly visited by tourists.
